steps.title: Recognize the pattern in the notes
steps.content: The notes present a broad claim—countries use animals, plants, or landforms on their flags—then provide specific examples to illustrate it (Grenada's nutmeg, Andorra's cow). An effective answer must follow this same structure: categorize flag symbols and back the category with a concrete example.

steps.title: Apply the pattern to the choices
steps.content: Choice C takes plant symbols as its category and provides Grenada's nutmeg as direct support. A confines itself to Grenada and asserts unsupported claims about national importance; B abandons symbols entirely to discuss vexillology; D announces a broad category without any supporting examples. That matches choice C — C is correct.
